data.content:
I can see you have a .NET 8 MSTest project. The express test expects `15.00` but the implementation returns `10.00`, so that one will fail.

I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use hot reload to iterate on failing tests. I can see the project is a .NET 8 MSTest project. The `dotnet watch test` command will re-run tests automatically when files change.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so the user can edit files and see results automatically.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-9573a80d7ca043148fbad02a07e71986 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content: The watcher is running but there's a build error — missing `using System;`. Let me fix that so the tests can actually run:
data.reasoningText:
There's a build error - `ArgumentException` needs `System` namespace. But the user asked me to set up hot reload, not fix their code. Let me note the error and let them know it's running. Actually, let me fix the missing `using System;` since it's blocking the watch from running at all, and it's a trivial fix.

Actually, the user said "I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster." So they want to iterate. The build error is blocking. Let me fix the missing `using` so the watch can actually run, then they can iterate on the actual test failure.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expected `15.00` but got `10.00`

The watcher is active in shell `watch`. Every time you save a file, tests will automatically re-run. To fix the failing test, update the `"express"` case in `CalculateShipping` from `10.00m` to `15.00m`. Want me to make that fix?
